changes.result: CONFIRMED — IDL REGISTRY IS ROOT CAUSE
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/interpretation_display_layer_v1/idl_records_v1.yaml:28-36 — ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1 has retail_display_label='Vascular Inflammation Risk' (line 31) and enabled_for_frontend=true (line 36). domain_narrative_wave1.py:303-312 (evidence_anchor_sentence) reads this dynamically via idl_record(). If vascular IDL resolves as primary_idl, card anchor reads 'Based mainly on: Vascular Inflammation Risk'. domain_narrative_wave1.py:400-413 (cv_contributor_primary) uses primary_idl only — IDL resolution order in domain_score_assembler.py:52 (_IDL_ORDER_CV) tries ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1 FIRST. MED-REV-1 hid the homocysteine subsystem at the health_system_card_evidence level but did NOT change enabled_for_frontend on the IDL record. Defect is live and traceable to idl_records_v1.yaml:36.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — INSULIN RESISTANCE IDL STILL ENABLED
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/interpretation_display_layer_v1/idl_records_v1.yaml:8-16 — ph_metabolic_early_ir_v1 has retail_display_label='Insulin Resistance Phenotype', subtitle='A pattern suggesting early impaired sugar and lipid handling', enabled_for_frontend=true (line 16). domain_narrative_wave1.py:486-513 (met_contributor_primary) will surface this IDL's subtitle if it is the resolved primary_idl. MED-REV-1 hid the insulin-resistance subsystem at the health_system_card level but did NOT change enabled_for_frontend on ph_metabolic_early_ir_v1. Blood sugar card can still anchor on insulin resistance context — defect is live.

backend/core/analytics/domain_narrative_wave1.py:350-352 — confidence_sentence_for('low', 'liver') returns: 'Confidence is limited — a fuller liver function panel (including GGT, ALP, albumin) would improve the read.' This text is hardcoded and does not check whether GGT, ALP, albumin are actually present on the panel. Liver confidence tier is derived from the biomarker coverage pool (_HEP_CONFIDENCE_POOL at domain_score_assembler.py:69-80). A 'low' tier only fires when panel coverage is thin — but the hardcoded copy may still incorrectly name GGT/ALP/albumin as missing when they are present but the overall tier is still low from other gaps. Defect is live.

backend/core/dto/result_versioning_policy_v1.py:107 — assess_result_versioning() returns regeneration_available=False hardcoded. No regeneration endpoint found in backend/app/routes/analysis.py (grep for 'regenerat' returned no matches). frontend/app/components/results/StaleResultBanner.tsx:38-43 — shows 'coming soon' placeholder text when regeneration_available=True, no button. REFERENCE_STALE_ANALYSIS_IDS at result_versioning_policy_v1.py:22-27 matches sprint UAT IDs exactly. Track B has real work to do.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — PREFLIGHT REQUIRED BEFORE ENDPOINT IMPLEMENTATION
changes.citation: backend/core/dto/persisted_replay_contract_v1.py:17-29 — PERSISTED_RENDER_REQUIRED_KEYS requires analysis_id, biomarkers, consumer_domain_scores, clinician_report_v1, narrative_report_v1, interpretation_display_layer_v1, replay_manifest, meta, result_version. backend/core/contracts/replay_manifest_v1.py:160-163 — lifestyle_input_hash field present but optional (default=None). Whether raw biomarker input is stored in a retrievable form is a runtime question that persistence_service.py and the DB schema must answer. Sprint preflight questions 1-8 (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:252-266) are the correct gate before endpoint commit.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:141-217 (Track A) and 222-306 (Track B) — allowed changes enumerated per domain. Layer A/B/C separation explicitly required at lines 310-318. Track A constraints list 8 explicit prohibitions (lines 203-217). Track B constraints list 8 prohibitions (lines 294-306). Scope is clearly bounded.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:405-419 — STOP conditions cover: Track A requiring scoring threshold changes (1), frontend medical inference (2), broad narrative compiler rewrite (3); Track B unable to locate stored input (4), overwriting old results (5), broad DB lineage migration (6), auditability gap (7), ARCH-RT-6 validator failure (8), scope drift into LLM translation (9). These comprehensively protect the Intelligence Core boundary.

changes.result: HARDENING NOTE — CURSOR MUST DECLARE AND REPORT FIX PATH
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/interpretation_display_layer_v1/idl_records_v1.yaml:36 — ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1 enabled_for_frontend=true. domain_score_assembler.py:52 — _IDL_ORDER_CV tries vascular IDL first. Cursor has two valid fix paths: (A) set enabled_for_frontend=false on ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1 in idl_records_v1.yaml (Layer A governed change — affects all IDL consumers); (B) change _IDL_ORDER_CV in domain_score_assembler.py to try lipid IDL first (Layer B logic change). Both are in scope for MIXED/HIGH. Cursor must declare which path is taken in the Track A root-cause findings section of the report, and must NOT apply both changes without explicit justification.

changes.result: HARDENING NOTE — TRACK A BLOOD SUGAR FIX MUST ADDRESS IDL
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/interpretation_display_layer_v1/idl_records_v1.yaml:16 — ph_metabolic_early_ir_v1 enabled_for_frontend=true. This IDL's subtitle ('A pattern suggesting early impaired sugar and lipid handling') can surface as the blood sugar card contributor via met_contributor_primary (domain_narrative_wave1.py:486-513). The same fix-path choice applies as for cardiovascular: (A) disable IDL for frontend in the YAML, or (B) change primary IDL resolution logic to exclude IR IDL from the blood sugar card anchor when insulin subsystem is hidden. Cursor must resolve this alongside the cardiovascular fix.

backend/core/analytics/domain_narrative_wave1.py:281-295 (confidence_sentence_cv_coherent) — adds homocysteine bridge language when 'homocysteine' appears in contributor text. If the vascular IDL fix suppresses homocysteine from the contributor path, this bridge will not fire. Cursor must confirm post-fix that confidence_sentence_cv_coherent no longer receives homocysteine-containing contributor text for the cardiovascular card. Confirm in Track A root-cause findings.

backend/core/analytics/domain_narrative_wave1.py:373-397 (cv_contributor, legacy) — greedy loop tries _ID_VASCULAR before _ID_LIPID. This function is superseded by cv_contributor_primary (D-6, lines 400-413). Cursor must not introduce any code path in domain_score_assembler.py that falls back to the legacy cv_contributor function. Confirm in Track A root-cause findings that assembler calls only cv_contributor_primary.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:228-241 — regeneration model: old result immutable, regenerated result is a new version or new record, old and new are linked or traceable. STOP condition 5 (line 412) fires if Track B would overwrite old results. backend/core/dto/result_versioning_policy_v1.py:116-150 (build_result_versioning_metadata) is read-only API — does not mutate stored results. Architecture is consistent with immutability requirement.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — IDL and narrative assembly are deterministic
changes.citation: domain_narrative_wave1.py is stateless and deterministic — all functions derive output from passed-in IDL records, signal rows, and bands. No LLM path. No random. The IDL YAML is a governed static artefact. Changing IDL enabled_for_frontend or IDL order in the assembler produces deterministic, governed output changes. Track B regeneration endpoint, if implemented, must invoke the existing orchestrator deterministically — STOP condition 4 protects against non-deterministic regeneration.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:318-319 — frontend must not infer clinical meaning, stale logic, or regeneration safety from raw fields; backend must provide safe metadata. STOP condition 2 (line 409) fires if Track A requires frontend medical inference. wave1HealthSystemCardDisplay.ts is presentational only (confirmed by reading file lines 1-49 — no clinical inference, only string formatting helpers). Cursor must maintain this boundary.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:341-373 — 6 Track A tests and 8 Track B tests enumerated. Mandatory commands: python backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py + python -m pytest backend/tests/architecture/test_day_one_architecture_guardrails.py -q. Track A regression tests should extend or reference backend/tests/regression/test_med_rev1_wave1_subsystem_visibility.py (created in MED-REV-1, confirmed in MED-REV-1 report).

Track A: ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1 enabled_for_frontend=true (idl_records_v1.yaml:36) with ordering priority over lipid IDL (domain_score_assembler.py:52) — live defect confirmed. liver confidence hardcoded copy at domain_narrative_wave1.py:350-352 — live defect confirmed. Track B: regeneration_available hardcoded False, no endpoint — real implementation required. Sprint has genuine work.

changes.result: NO STOP CONDITIONS TRIGGERED IN HARDENING
changes.citation: Pre-hardening assessment: (1) Track A fixes are copy/IDL alignment — no scoring threshold changes; (2) no frontend clinical inference required; (3) no broad narrative compiler rewrite — assembler IDL order or IDL registry change; (4) Track B preflight is advisory before endpoint commit — not pre-triggered; (5) immutability rule is stated and protected by STOP condition 5; (6) DB migration requirement unknown — STOP condition 6 protects; (7) auditability gap protected by STOP condition 7; (8) ARCH-RT-6 validator scope is architecture guardrails, not card copy — should remain green; (9) no scope drift to LLM translation. All STOP conditions clear in hardening phase.

Sprint explicitly prohibits (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:203-217, 294-306): clinical scoring thresholds, rail score calculations, marker values, SignalEvaluator, SignalRegistry, root-cause compiler logic, PSI status, reintroducing hidden subsystems as visible scored evidence, frontend clinical inference, overwriting existing result payloads, mutating old analysis records, fallback parsers, broad DB migrations. No forbidden file paths are referenced as targets in the sprint body.
Sprint prompt references no .codex/, .vscode/, or AGENTS.md files. No tooling leakage risk identified.
